mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate declines signal monetary easing that reduces discount rates applied to equity valuations. A 25 basis point or greater drop in FEDFUNDS over a 3-month window triggers positive S&P 500 returns within the subsequent 20 trading days as investors reprice equity risk premiums downward.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS declines >= 25 basis points over preceding 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 5.25% as of 2007-05-01 represents peak tightening cycle
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in fewer than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window post-trigger.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained payroll employment growth above 150,000 jobs per month signals robust labor market demand and consumer spending capacity. This always-on trend predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as earnings expectations rise with employment-driven consumption.
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS momentum and 60-day forward returns falls below 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window, or correlation reverses to negative for more than 2 consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly signal consumer and construction sector demand. When both series decline simultaneously by more than 5% month-over-month, forward equity returns turn negative within 20 trading days as growth expectations contract and earnings revisions become negative.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and RSAFS both decline > 5% month-over-month in same month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,528 thousand and RSAFS at 377,885 million as of 2007-04-01 and 2007-05-01
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in fewer than 50% of joint-decline triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth decelerates below 0.5% month-over-month, signaling supply-side weakness and potential margin compression. This threshold-triggered condition predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as investors reprrice earnings expectations downward in response to capacity utilization concerns.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO deceleration and 5-day forward returns is below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or the mechanism fails to predict negative returns in more than 45% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL accelerates above 2.5% year-over-year while FEDFUNDS remains below 5.5%, creating a real rate compression that signals monetary policy lag. This regime-conditional interaction predicts negative equity returns over 20 trading days as the market anticipates future rate hikes and earnings margin erosion from input cost inflation.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 207.387 and FEDFUNDS at 5.25% as of 2007-05-01
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in fewer than 52% of triggered instances, or the interaction term coefficient is not significant (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises by 0.3 percentage points or more over a 3-month window, signaling labor market deterioration and reduced consumer confidence. This threshold-triggered condition predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as earnings expectations decline with reduced employment and spending.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises >= 0.3 percentage points over preceding 3 months
failure_condition: Rank IC between UNRATE increases and 60-day forward returns is below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or negative returns occur in fewer than 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly decelerates to below 2% annualized rate while RSAFS simultaneously contracts month-over-month. This interaction signals demand-side recession risk, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as earnings revisions turn negative across cyclical sectors.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth < 2% AND RSAFS month-over-month decline > 1%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11,531.7 billion and RSAFS at 377,885 million as of 2007-01-01 and 2007-05-01
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in fewer than 58% of joint-trigger instances, or the interaction coefficient fails to achieve statistical significance (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) maintains consistent month-over-month growth above 0.3% while PAYEMS employment growth remains positive, signaling sustained supply-side capacity expansion. This always-on trend predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as productivity gains support margin expansion and earnings growth.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 112.6889 and PAYEMS at 137,831 thousand as of 2007-05-01
failure_condition: Rank IC of the joint INDPRO-PAYEMS signal with 5-day forward returns falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in fewer than 60% of periods when both conditions are met.
